name: Modified low-N ASP-2, N-deprived, pure Ar sparge (no CO2) (PMC3419522)
description: Modified low-N ASP-2, N-deprived, pure Ar sparge (no CO2) — medium reported in PMC3419522, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC3419522 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.formulation_warning: workflow correction sparse; kept original — This is a physiological perturbation/starvation state (H2-induction), not a growth-supporting formulation, but its salt base is the defined modified low-N ASP-2 medium, so I corrected rather than rejected it. Corrections vs auto-extract: (1) REMOVED ammonium — the condition is N-deprived because medium influx is interrupted, so no external N is supplied; (2) NO CO2 (pure Ar sparge, no CO2) so there is no external carbon source; (3) REMOVED O2 and set oxygen=anaerobic — pure-Ar sparge makes the culture anoxic, which is required for H2 evolution; (4) iron is FeCl3 = Fe3+, not Fe2+. The auto-extract's separate Co2+ is part of the ASP-2 P-II trace-metal base (folded into the base component). Full ASP-2 seawater base salts / trace metals / vitamin B12 amounts are in ref 34 (Van Baalen 1962), not quantified here — listed presence-only, not fabricated.
provenance.verification_evidence: "modified low-N ASP-2 containing 0.75 mM NH4Cl"; N-deprivation: "interruption of medium influx to the bioreactor" / "H2 became detectable within 1 h upon the perturbation"; gas: alternative condition with "pure Ar" atmosphere (no CO2).
